HPI: This is a 72 year old female who:
returns for a follow up regarding the right knee.
returned despite two steroid injections, first in November 2025 and her second in January 2026.
states that now, she is requiring assistance going from a sitting to standing position secondary to her
She also feels she is losing range of motion over time.
read about a jiffy knee procedure and inquires if this is an option today.
Exam findings notable for the following:
X-rays: 4 views of the right knee were taken in the office on 10/29/2025.
Imaging: MRI right knee done at Pueblo Medical Imaging on 11/4/2025 demonstrates moderate/severe
tricompartmental osteoarthritis worst in the medial compartment with bone-on-bone phenomena.
Medial extrusion of the body of the medial meniscus due to osteoarthritis and joint space loss.
Patchy bone marrow edema within the distal
femur and proximal tibia which is presumably degeneration in nature.
ULTRASOUND: Limited ultrasound right knee done in the office on 11/12/2025.

Orthobiologics refers to products such as tissue, cells, and molecules made by those cells called growth factors that may aid in
Autologous platelet rich plasma (PRP) is the component of blood that has been prepared to maximize the concentration of platelets
The PRP can be introduced into the painful joint or soft tissue to wake up the signaling
between these molecules necessary for healing.
Autologous means that your own cells are used, eliminating much of the concern
regarding disease transmission or allergic reaction.
PRP can be obtained from a standard blood draw.
the potential benefits of PRP for some tendon and joint disorders.
Some growth factors are used in orthopedic surgery to aid the healing of bone and soft tissue structures.
Stem cells are cells that have the potential to turn into different types, or lineages, of cells, such as the cells that make up muscle,
Stem cells produce and release molecules called growth factors that participate in communication
The richest source of stem cells in the body is the bone marrow.
There are basic science studies that support the
ability of stem cells to help with healing and repair.
There is ongoing research to establish the efficacy of stem cell delivery at the

desired PRP cells into a richly concentrated layer.
